21.correct: The Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) were borrowed from the Constitution of Ireland.

wrong.A: From the UK, India borrowed the parliamentary system and rule of law.
wrong.B: From Canada came the federal structure with a strong centre.
wrong.C: From the USA came Fundamental Rights and judicial review.

21.fact: The DPSP are non-justiciable guidelines for the State, contained in Part IV of the Constitution.

22.correct: Among the options, Bihar has a bicameral legislature (a Legislative Assembly and a Legislative Council).

wrong.A: Haryana has only a Legislative Assembly.
wrong.C: Himachal Pradesh has only a Legislative Assembly.

22.fact: Only a few states (like Bihar, UP, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh) have a Legislative Council.

23.correct: The Indian Census classifies the working population into three categories: main workers, marginal workers and non-workers.

wrong.B: Agricultural/industrial/service is a sectoral split, not the census worker classification.
wrong.C: Organised/unorganised/unemployed is not the census categorisation.
wrong.D: Rural/urban/migrant is not the worker classification used.

23.fact: A 'main worker' works for at least 183 days (6 months) in a year; below that one is a 'marginal worker'.

24.correct: The Food Safety and Standards Act, 2006 created a separate authority (FSSAI) to regulate food safety in India.

wrong.B: The National Food Security Act, 2013 deals with subsidised food grains, not the food-safety regulator.
wrong.C: The Competition Act, 2002 set up the Competition Commission.
wrong.D: The Consumer Protection Act, 2019 deals with consumer rights.

24.fact: The FSSAI sets food standards and oversees food safety across India.

25.correct: India's Green Revolution used high-yielding variety (HYV) seeds mainly of rice and wheat, dramatically increasing food-grain output.

wrong.B: Sugarcane and tea were not the focus of HYV seeds in the Green Revolution.
wrong.C: Jowar and bajra (millets) were not the main HYV crops then.
wrong.D: Mustard and ragi were not the Green Revolution's lead crops.

25.fact: M.S. Swaminathan is regarded as the father of India's Green Revolution.

26.correct: Both pairs are correct: Shalabhoga (I) was land for the maintenance of a school, and Devadana/Tirunamattukkani (II) was land gifted to temples - so 'Both I and II' is right.

wrong.A: 'Only I' omits the correct temple-land term.
wrong.B: 'Only II' omits the correct school-land term.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong as both pairs are correctly matched.

26.fact: Chola inscriptions record several land categories, reflecting a well-organised rural economy.

27.correct: The 'Bal Vivah Mukt Bharat' (Child Marriage Free India) campaign was launched in November 2024 by Annapurna Devi, the Union Minister for Women and Child Development.

wrong.A: Smriti Irani was no longer the WCD Minister at that time.
wrong.B: Amit Shah did not launch this campaign.
wrong.C: The campaign was launched by the WCD Minister, not directly by the Prime Minister.

27.fact: India aims to eliminate child marriage, which remains a challenge in several states.

28.correct: The famous bronze 'Dancing Girl' figurine was discovered at Mohenjo-daro, a major Indus Valley Civilization site.

wrong.A: Lothal was a dockyard town but not where the Dancing Girl was found.
wrong.D: Ropar (Rupnagar) is an Indus site but not where this figure was found.

28.fact: The Dancing Girl, made by the lost-wax casting method, shows the metallurgical skill of the Harappans.

29.correct: To impeach the President, the motion must be signed by at least one-fourth of the total membership of the House in which it is initiated.

wrong.A: There is no fixed figure of 100 Lok Sabha members for this.
wrong.B: There is no fixed figure of 50 Rajya Sabha members.
wrong.D: It is one-fourth to initiate (and a two-thirds majority to pass), not half.

29.fact: The President can be impeached only for 'violation of the Constitution' (Article 61).

30.correct: The hypothalamus releases growth-hormone-releasing factor (hormone), which stimulates the pituitary to secrete growth hormone.

wrong.B: The thyroid gland secretes thyroxine, not growth-hormone-releasing factor.
wrong.C: The pituitary secretes growth hormone in response to the hypothalamus.
wrong.D: The thymus is involved in immunity (T-cell maturation).

30.fact: The hypothalamus links the nervous and endocrine systems by controlling the pituitary gland.

31.correct: 'Laru Kaj' is a traditional festival celebrated by the Gond community of Madhya Pradesh.

wrong.A: It is not a Bhil festival.
wrong.B: It is not a Baiga festival.
wrong.D: It is not a Sahariya festival.

31.fact: The Gonds are one of the largest tribal communities in central India.

32.correct: The Guru Ghasidas-Tamor Pingla Tiger Reserve (Chhattisgarh) was notified as India's 56th tiger reserve.

wrong.B: Bandhavgarh is an older, established tiger reserve.
wrong.C: Sundarbans is a long-established tiger reserve.
wrong.D: Kanha is one of the oldest tiger reserves.

32.fact: Tiger reserves are managed under Project Tiger, launched in 1973.

33.correct: Both the heart (I) and the gallbladder (II) ARE routinely imaged using ultrasound (echocardiography and abdominal ultrasound), so neither is an organ that is not imaged - hence 'Neither I nor II'.

wrong.A: 'Only I' is wrong because the heart is imaged by ultrasound.
wrong.B: 'Only II' is wrong because the gallbladder is imaged by ultrasound.
wrong.C: 'Both' would mean both are not imaged, which is incorrect.

33.fact: Ultrasound (sonography) uses high-frequency sound waves to image soft tissues and organs.

34.correct: Personal Disposable Income (PDI) = Personal Income minus personal (direct) tax payments minus non-tax payments (such as fees/fines to government).

wrong.A: It subtracts non-tax payments too, not adds them.
wrong.C: PDI starts from personal income, not national income.
wrong.D: PDI is not national income minus those items.

34.fact: Disposable income is what households can actually spend or save after compulsory payments.

35.correct: The first Indian Open Golf Championship was held in 1964.

wrong.B: 1978 is later than the first edition.

35.fact: The Indian Open is one of Asia's prominent professional golf tournaments.

36.correct: India and Bhutan held the 6th Joint Group of Customs meeting at Thimphu from 24 to 25 April 2025.

wrong.B: It was not held at Gandhinagar.
wrong.C: It was not held at Paro.
wrong.D: It was not held in New Delhi.

36.fact: Thimphu is the capital of Bhutan, India's close Himalayan neighbour.

37.correct: The book 'Indian Wisdom' (in this context) is authored by Devdutt Pattanaik, known for his works on mythology.

wrong.A: Sadhguru writes on yoga and spirituality (e.g. 'Inner Engineering').
wrong.B: Deepak Chopra writes on mind-body wellness.
wrong.D: Radhakrishnan Pillai writes on Chanakya's wisdom.

37.fact: Devdutt Pattanaik has written many bestselling books retelling Indian myths.

38.correct: The RESET (Retired Sportsperson Empowerment Training) programme was launched in India in 2024 to help former athletes build second careers.

wrong.D: 2023 is not when it was launched.

38.fact: The scheme trains retired sportspersons in coaching, management and allied roles.

39.correct: Sweden ranked first in the Energy Transition Index 2025, reflecting its progress towards clean and secure energy.

wrong.B: Finland ranked high but not first.
wrong.C: Denmark ranked among the leaders but not first.
wrong.D: The USA did not top the index.

39.fact: The Energy Transition Index is published by the World Economic Forum.

40.correct: In the District SDG Index 2023-24, all districts of both Sikkim (I) and Mizoram (II) were classified as 'Front Runners', so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both states' districts were Front Runners.

40.fact: The SDG India Index tracks progress on the Sustainable Development Goals; 'Front Runner' is a high-performing category.
